from pathlib import Path
roots = [Path('global'), Path('core'), Path('impl'), Path('logger'), Path('loader'), Path('resource_cooker'), Path('Testbed')]
exts = {'.h', '.cpp'}
problems = []
for root in roots:
    if not root.exists():
        continue
    for p in root.rglob('*'):
        if not p.is_file() or p.suffix not in exts:
            continue
        if '3rd_parties' in p.parts:

        data = p.read_bytes()
        label = str(p)

        if not data_no_bom.startswith(b'// limztudio@gmail.com\\r\\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\\r\\n'):
            problems.append((label, 'missing/incorrect banner or first separator'))
        if b'\\n' in data_no_bom.replace(b'\\r\\n', b''):
            problems.append((label, 'LF-only or mixed line endings'))
        if not data_no_bom.endswith(b'////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\\r\\n\\r\\n'):
            problems.append((label, 'incorrect EOF separator/newline convention'))
for label, issue in problems:
    print(f'{label}: {issue}')
if not problems:
    print('source encoding/CRLF/banner/EOF checks passed')

Long slash separator lines must stay flush-left; inside class/struct/function scopes, use triple-newline category spacing instead of indented slash separators.

Multiline `if` conditions place the outer closing `)`/`))` at `if` indentation; braced bodies attach `{` to that closer line (`){` or `)){`), and final inline lambda arguments keep the closing parentheses on the lambda closing-brace line (for example `}))`, or `})){` with a braced body).
